Vulnerabilities.Title: openssh: regreSSHion - Race Condition in signal handler
Vulnerabilities.Description: A signal handler race condition was found in OpenSSH's server (sshd). If a client does not authenticate within LoginGraceTime seconds, sshd's SIGALRM handler is called asynchronously — but this signal handler calls various functions that are not async-signal-safe.

Vulnerabilities.Title: glibc: out-of-bounds write in iconv may lead to RCE
Vulnerabilities.Description: The iconv() function in the GNU C Library versions 2.39 and older may overflow the output buffer passed to it by up to 4 bytes when converting strings to the ISO-2022-CN-EXT character set.

Vulnerabilities.Title: glibc: stack-based buffer overflow in netgroup cache
cryptography: NULL pointer dereference in PKCS12 parsing
cryptography: NULL dereference preparing a PKCS#12 key and certificate
OpenSSL: RFC7250 handshakes with unauthenticated servers — clients may skip server verification
openssl: denial of service via null dereference

Misconfigurations.Title: Image user should not be 'root'
Misconfigurations.Description: Running containers as root is a significant security risk. If code execution happens in the container, an attacker has root level access.
Misconfigurations.Message: Specify a user in the Dockerfile: USER <non-root-user>
Misconfigurations.Resolution: Add 'USER <non root user name>' to the Dockerfile

Misconfigurations.Title: Add no new privileges
Misconfigurations.Description: Docker best practice is to set the no-new-privileges flag to prevent the container from gaining new privileges during execution.

Misconfigurations.Title: No HEALTHCHECK defined
Misconfigurations.Description: You should add a HEALTHCHECK instruction to your Dockerfile to allow container orchestration to determine container status.

Misconfigurations.Title: RUN using 'sudo' is not allowed
Misconfigurations.Description: Use of sudo is not allowed in a Dockerfile. Use the USER instruction instead.

Lines.Content: RUN sudo apt-get install -y curl wget netcat

Misconfigurations.Title: Process can elevate its own privileges
Misconfigurations.Description: A program inside the container can elevate its own privileges and run as root, which might give the program control over the container and node.
Misconfigurations.Message: Container 'payment-api' of Deployment 'payment-service' should set 'securityContext.allowPrivilegeEscalation' to false

Misconfigurations.Title: Default capabilities not dropped
Misconfigurations.Description: The container should drop all default capabilities and add only those that are needed for its execution.
Misconfigurations.Message: Container 'payment-api' of Deployment 'payment-service' should add 'ALL' to 'securityContext.capabilities.drop'

Misconfigurations.Title: CPU not limited
Misconfigurations.Description: Enforcing CPU limits prevents DoS via resource exhaustion.

Misconfigurations.Title: Root filesystem is not read-only
Misconfigurations.Description: An immutable root filesystem can prevent malicious binaries being added to PATH and increase attack cost.
Misconfigurations.Message: Container 'payment-api' of Deployment 'payment-service' should set 'securityContext.readOnlyRootFilesystem' to true

Misconfigurations.Title: Runs with low user ID
Misconfigurations.Description: Force the container to run with user ID > 10000 to avoid conflicts with the host's user table.
